violations.description: Suite-level serial mode limits parallel safety and can mask inter-test coupling issues.
violations.suggestion: Remove serial mode and isolate shared state with per-test setup/cleanup.

violations.description: API automate suite is forced to serial execution, reducing confidence in isolation under parallel CI workers.
violations.suggestion: Run in parallel by default and retain serial only for proven hard dependencies.

violations.description: Tests mutate process-wide env flags, which can leak behavior across concurrently executed tests if scope expands.
violations.suggestion: Centralize env mutation in scoped helpers and ensure strict restoration per test block.

recommendations: Eliminate blanket serial mode and validate suites under parallel workers.
Minimize process-global state mutation in route integration tests.
Keep explicit setup/teardown ownership near each mutated integration surface.
summary: Isolation has 3 violations with 2 HIGH risks centered on serial execution mode.
